examples.english: He praised him for his actions and his noble character.

examples.text: لَا تَحۡسَبَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَفۡرَحُونَ بِمَاۤ أَتَوا۟ وَّيُحِبُّونَ أَن يُحۡمَدُوا۟ بِمَا لَمۡ يَفۡعَلُوا۟ فَلَا تَحۡسَبَنَّهُم بِمَفَازَةࣲ مِّنَ الْعَذَابِ ۖ وَلَهُمۡ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمࣱ
examples.english: Do not think that those who joy in what they carry out and love to be praised for what they do not do will escape torment. Theirs is [indeed] an agonizing torment.

senses.gloss: to praise, to commend; to praise or exalt (a deity)
to praise, to commend; to praise (someone) as a sign of gratitude; to thank
to praise, to commend; to find (something or someone) pleasant or agreeable; to approve; to admire

examples.english: He approved of his deeds and beliefs.

senses.gloss: to praise highly or much, to extol, to exalt
